post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 156: To Slay a God
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
[This flavor is a continuation of Mafia Game 152. It is strongly recommended to read the flavor there first - you do not need to read the entire game.]

A world was once created.

It struggled, at first, through a battle with demons. But the forces of good eventually prevailed, as is in every story, and the world expanded. Before long, it expanded to the size of a fully-grown world, complete with its own rules.

The rules governing a world are wholly dependent on what the Sentients of the world make of it. However, if they neglect to account for something, it can be shaped by mortals. This is the fundamental truth behind magic, and it is one of the most difficult goals of a mortal because the Sentients will often fix their errors once they are caught.

The Research Facility in Cera Seles called itself "for the advancement of science", but really, it was just trying to figure out exactly what the Sentients missed. The "scientists" there should have been called magicians, discovering exactly what the Sentients missed and attempting to exploit it for as long as possible before it was patched. The patches usually took longer nowadays than before, when there were more Sentients. Some of them were forced out, or left, due to... various reasons. There were four then, and they could not deal with every instance of rule breakage as it happened.


So when Chief Scientist Sustrai, the administrator of the Research Facility, requested a Sentient to demonstrate a huge oversight that needed fixing, we should have suspected malicious intent, but did not. A Sentient visited the Research Facility that day, and never returned. And Sustrai appeared to have obtained the power of a Sentient. This was magic of the most worldshaking kind.


We, of course, needed to find the scientists who had discovered this exploit, and silence them before they could subjugate us to their world. Let us hope we can discover the scientists before it is too late.



...

I had to enter myself. There was far too much at stake here for me to leave this to my subordinates. Stupid beings...

I will arrive and begin to control this world. Soon enough, I will be able to conquer this world... in the name of the Demonic Empire.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderators, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. Ties will be explained in the next section. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • If there is a tie at the end of the day, the player who first obtained that number of votes first will be lynched. If a player is unvoted, this will reset.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is. If we do not notice that you fakevoted, it may be counted as a real vote, so it is a good idea to PM me every time you intend to fakevote.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity
  • Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
  • Each night will be 4 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • Activity rule for this game: While not on V/LA, you are expected to make at least one game-advancing post between every two activity checks, which will be done roughly every 48 hours. So that I know you have read the rules, say so in your Role PM. (Game-advancing is lenient, and is there to prevent posts like "activity post".) If you fail to meet this requirement, you will be given a warning, and afterwards, will be replaced.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, and SE’s/IC's up to one. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force-replaced, depending on the scenario. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (summitwei) am the Moderator of Game 156. Kagebaka is the co-moderator of Game 156.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks. It is also used for 3rd party flavor-related posts.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This is a semi-closed setup. There are three parties: The Town, Mafia, and one 3rd party role; however, the 3rd party's wincon and abilities are not given.
The starting setup is: 6 VT's, 3 Town PR's, 2 Mafia Goons, 1 Mafia PR, 1 3rd Party. The four base power roles are: Protective, Investigative, Killing, and Manipulative (Roleblocker, etc.) It is not given which role is the Mafia PR or what the exact roles are. The roles of the game may change over time - read the Special Mechanics section for more info regarding this.

Roleclaiming and flavorclaiming are explicitly allowed. However, all non-VT's are given VT fakeclaims, and the Mafia PR is given a Town PR fakeclaim. You are discouraged from trying to speculate about these claims.

All PM's are unique in that their flavortexts are different. Do not quote them or anything of that sort, though the risk is minimal.


Special Mechanics
This game has a special mechanic, and it governs solely the power roles of this game. Power roles in this game are fluid. They can be transferred in any of the following ways:

-At night, a player may choose to give their power role to another. If this action cannot go through, then nothing will happen.
-‎If a player is lynched, the first person on their lynching wagon will receive the power role. If this player is ineligible to receive the role, it will go to the second person on the wagon, and so on. If all players on the wagon have been exhausted, the ability will go to a randomly selected powerless player.
-‎If a player with a power role is nightkilled, their ability goes to a randomly selected powerless player.
-‎The Mafia in this game have an ability to take a power role from another player. If this ability is successful, the power role is transferred to the Mafia who uses this action. This action will occur before the above action where a player gifts their power to another in night action priority.
-‎A player may not have more than one power role at once.
-‎If a player would have gotten a power role but dies at the same time, the ability will be removed from the game.
-‎Players who start out as power roles will have generally stronger abilities than players who started out as vanilla roles. All starting non-PR's have the same ability with the same base power role, and all starting PR's have the same (strictly stronger) ability with each base power role.


Alive (2)
  1. idomath12345 (Town Sentient, survived in victory)
  2. atomicpianowitch (Town Sentient, survived in victory)


Dead (11)

TeddyYT, Town Sentient, was lynched Day 2 *

sudzoof, Vanilla Town, was lynched Day 3. *

mathjay, Mafia Goon, was lynched Day 4. *
jeffisepic, Vanilla Town, was killed Night 4. *
SFGiantsFan, Mafia Sentient, was lynched Day 5. *
e_is_cool, 3rd Party Depowerer, completed his wincondition Night 5. *


Significant Events
KiwiYum replaces abean077 *
Day 1 ends *
Night 1 ends *
Charge is killed *
sudzoof replaces Madelyn *
Fvr_Vain replaces Potterx *
Day 2 ends *

math_turtle37 is killed *
mathjay replaces bigmath *

jeffisepic replaces Fvr_Vain *
Night 3 ends *
Someone is shot Day 4 - nobody dies *
Day 4 ends *
Night 4 ends *
Day 5 ends *
Night 5 ends, town and 3rd party co-win *


Warnings
None so far!

post_rendered:
Flavor
It was going to be a simple meeting. One of some of the most important people in the world, sure, but a simple meeting nonetheless.
All that was intended was to generate a plan for discovering the rogue scientists, who had appeared to have merged into society.


Then we received word that some of our friends were not who they said they were. Some of the other Sentients seemed to have taken some places, putting their fate into their own hands. But more urgently, the scientists who we were directly working to bring down seemed to have joined us at some point.

The flow of the conversation changed immediately. We needed to find the scientists now, before they took control of everything. And other meddling forces could only make this more difficult...


It is now Day 1. You may post. Day 1 ends at 9:00 PM EST on 1/30/18.

post_rendered:
We have Players A to M, in this game. J is third Party, K, L, M are mafia. A, B and C are Power Roles, from most useful to least useful.

Lynching:

We lynch I. A dies. We lynch H. B dies. We lynch G. C dies. Then, it is LyLo, assuming J is a good third party member, like Survivor. We have D, E, F and J(Hopefully), for the Village.

If we no lynch twice, we are left with E, F and G and hopefully J to find who the Mafia are. That’s worse, because these people are less experienced.

Also, in the worst case, if J is cult, that means that Day 2 we have to lynch J if we lynched, whereas we have to lynch J Day 3 if we no lynch. The first strategy grants us a mislynch is we lynch town, and if we lynch mafia the first three days we have to lynch J Day 4.

Thus, it can be concluded that lynching Day 1 is much better than no-lynching Day 1.

Thanks Kevin for guiding me through Mafia Game 140 before he was banned.

Story
The Republic of Efnas was notorious among the other regions for its corrupt politicians. They would always argue back that it is impossible to be corrupt when the corruption is built into the system. The country always functioned smoothly; its leaders would come and go as the balance of power shifted (but never by too much), and the capitalist economy was regulated. The country itself rarely had issues, aside from foreign relations. In such a world with such radically different nations, disagreements in foreign relations were to be expected. Lipenga, however, clashed more than most, but he would never have been brought to deathpoint.

That all changed when it was revealed that some of the people in the meeting were impostors. Perhaps this was the perfect place to throw the others' annoyance out on him, but before long, he had been killed - ironic, the "corrupt" politician being lynched by his "pure" comrades.


Lipenga was elected one year ago, and the amount of good he had done for his country was immeasurable. But all good leaders must die.

Just not always at the hands of the others.

post_rendered:
Story
The four nations were always in conflict. However, their closeness allowed for a unique concept - those who wished to travel between them could freely do so. With enough money, anybody who disagreed with their own country's ideals could travel to another, which would have a method of rule they would feel more affinity to.

The nations, in addition, were strictly disallowed by Sera Celes, the connecting force between them, to quarrel with the other nations in anything other than words. A war between nations would, obviously, be disastrous. Something even more covert, like assassination, would be unthinkable. Lipenga's death, if released to the general public, would spark an outrage.

We were, admittedly, relieved, when we began the summit the next day, nothing out of the ordinary.

No one died.

post_rendered:
Story
An Ascension leaves behind a bit of residue transuniversal energy. The energy, while not nearly sufficient for another full Ascension, can cause some... interesting occurrences. Combined with strong ties to the world to which the Ascension went to, it is theoretically possible for one to travel to the other world.

But this is theoretical. At least, for the most part.


There are very rare, and undocumented, cases of this actually occurring. The Travellers, as they are called, are given premier status, but their existence as being not of this world is covered up. They are promised that they would be helped in whatever way possible, that they would be able to find a way home.


Being killed in the middle of the most crucial meeting of the world would not be what is colloquially referred to as "help", but who knows.

Charge has died. She was a Traveller (Vanilla Town).


Day continues. The deadline is still 2/17/18 at 9:30 PM EST.

Flavor
Cera Seles' University taught many various things. Among the list were science-related academics, literature, history, and, of course, politics. The world had always been divided into four major regions - one for each of the four Sentients still alive after the Great War. Each nation had its issues, but they were easy to overlook if you lived there, or wanted to live there.

The University used Polma as its epitome of social class structure. It came with its downsides, of course, but optimally, everybody worked towards the betterment of society. The Kingdom had been created soon after the Great War, seven hundred twenty-four years ago, by Polmik.

Polmik himself was somewhat reclusive. Shortly after the creation and setup of his nation, he set up camp thousands of miles away to pursue his own endeavors. But he would always return for crises, and this certainly qualified as one.


Though he never expected to pass, the summit could march on without him.

TeddyYT was lynched. He was Polmik (Town Sentient).


It is now Night 2. Do not post. Night 2 ends on 2/22/18 at 8:00 PM EST. Note the extension of 22 hours - this is both for the V/LA's, and so that I can deal with ever-later phase times more easily.

post_rendered:
Flavor
The Research Facility itself was one of the later constructions in the timeline of Cera Seles. It was established well after the war that removed three Sentients out of the world, and was originally for actual science. It was only after Sustrai's promotion to Chief Scientist that the Facility began exploiting loopholes in the fabric of society.

Some of these loopholes were beneficial to society. Scientist Oldyn, for instance, discovered a way to bypass gravity, and managed to make quite a bit of money before the trick was removed. This was the first instance of such an exploit being used for profit, and thus, the new cycle of the Research Facility was born. The Sentients, as much as they hated it, could not do anything about the scientists, and so they continued to reap the benefits of their exploits.

Scientist Oldyn was found dead in his room before we started the summit. His contributions to society were... sketchy at best, but we had no doubt that one of our impostors had been taken care of. Only a few left, we figured, and this madness would be over.

ShineBunny has died. He was Scientist Oldyn (Mafia Goon).


It is now Day 3. You may post. Day 3 ends on 3/8/18 at 8:10 PM EST.

Flavor
Cera Seles' University taught many various things. Among the list were science-related academics, literature, history, and, of course, politics. The world had always been divided into four major regions - one for each of the four Sentients still alive after the Great War. Each nation had its issues, but they were easy to overlook if you lived there, or wanted to live there.

The University used Polma as its epitome of social class structure. It came with its downsides, of course, but optimally, everybody worked towards the betterment of society. The Kingdom had been created soon after the Great War, seven hundred twenty-four years ago, by Polmik.

Polmik himself was somewhat reclusive. Shortly after the creation and setup of his nation, he set up camp thousands of miles away to pursue his own endeavors. But he would always return for crises, and this certainly qualified as one.


Though he never expected to pass, the summit could march on without him.

summitwei wrote:
Flavor
The Research Facility itself was one of the later constructions in the timeline of Cera Seles. It was established well after the war that removed three Sentients out of the world, and was originally for actual science. It was only after Sustrai's promotion to Chief Scientist that the Facility began exploiting loopholes in the fabric of society.

Some of these loopholes were beneficial to society. Scientist Oldyn, for instance, discovered a way to bypass gravity, and managed to make quite a bit of money before the trick was removed. This was the first instance of such an exploit being used for profit, and thus, the new cycle of the Research Facility was born. The Sentients, as much as they hated it, could not do anything about the scientists, and so they continued to reap the benefits of their exploits.

Scientist Oldyn was found dead in his room before we started the summit. His contributions to society were... sketchy at best, but we had no doubt that one of our impostors had been taken care of. Only a few left, we figured, and this madness would be over.

ShineBunny has died. He was Scientist Oldyn (Mafia Goon).

Searching: Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then fullcop this player - that is, you will learn their exact role, and any abilities they may have at the moment.
Demonic Presence (passive): Whenever you gain a base power role, you lose it and it is immediately removed from the game.
Prolonged Survival (passive): The game will not end until either you are dead or your wincon has been achieved. If your wincon is somehow achieved after you are dead, you will still win.

Telepathy (passive): You do not physically do the Depowerment action. Demonic Biology (passive): You show up as a Mafia Goon for all investigative purposes.
Another World (passive): You are immune to the Mafia nightkill. You are not, however, immune to the kill by the Vigilante role.

Alignment Cop: You will be notified of the alignment of the player.
Rolecop: You will be informed of the player's role. This will include the current abilities the player may have. Note that VT's and Goons have the same result (which is Vanilla).
Track: You will be informed of who, if anybody, the player targeted.
Watch: You will be informed of what actions, if any, the player performed.
